structure.title: Federal Register Final Rule Header & Administrative Metadata — 42 CFR Part 411 (CMS–1720–F) + Backgr
section_path: Federal Register Final Rule Header & Administrative Metadata — 42 CFR Part 411 (CMS–1720–F)

metadata.summary: Identifies the issuing agency (CMS/HHS), regulatory part (42 CFR Part 411), rule identifier (CMS–1720–F), and rule type (Final rule). Captures the rule’s high-level purpose (modernizing/clarifying physician self-referral regulations), key exceptions being established/amended, and effective date(s) including the delayed effective date for an amendment to § 411.352(i).

structure.title: Regulatory History Chronology — Major Stark Rulemakings (1992–2007: Proposed Rules; Phases I–III) +
section_path: Regulatory History Chronology — Major Stark Rulemakings (1992–2007: Proposed Rules; Phases I–III)

metadata.summary: Provides a chronological account of key Stark rulemaking milestones: 1992 and 1998 proposed rules; 1995 final rule; and the staged final rule process (Phase I 2001, Phase II 2004 with omitted portion later published, Phase III 2007). Includes Federal Register citations/dates that are frequently used as interpretive history for compliance positions.

structure.title: CY 2019 PFS Substantive Update — Writing Requirement via “Collection of Documents” and Signature Non
section_path: CY 2019 PFS Substantive Update — Writing Requirement via “Collection of Documents” and Signature Noncompliance Rule Changes

metadata.summary: Codifies CMS’s policy that Stark compensation arrangement writing requirements in § 411.357 can be satisfied through a collection of documents (including contemporaneous course-of-conduct documentation). Updates § 411.353(g) temporary noncompliance with signature requirements by removing the “once every 3 years” limitation and conforming to SSA § 1877(h)(1)(E).

structure.title: Policy Considerations — Historical Medicare Fee-for-Service Volume Incentives at Time of Stark Enactment (FFS, DRGs/IPPS) (part 1/191)
section_path: Policy Considerations — Historical Medicare Fee-for-Service Volume Incentives at Time of Stark Enactment (FFS, DRGs/IPPS)

metadata.summary: Describes the payment context in 1989 when Stark was enacted: traditional Medicare Parts A and B were largely volume-driven fee-for-service, with some bundling such as DRG-based inpatient payments under IPPS. Sets up the contrast between historical single-provider bundling versus emerging multi-provider/value-based models, framing the need to modernize Stark exceptions.

metadata.summary: Describes the payment context in 1989 when Stark was enacted: traditional Medicare Parts A and B were largely volume-driven fee-for-service, with some bundling such as DRG-based inpatient payments under IPPS. Sets up the contrast between historical single-provider bundling versus emerging multi-provider/value-based models, framing the need to modernize Stark exceptions.
